meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solve Design Gaps with AI Project Management
content.seo_description: Bridge your design gap as a freelancer. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end project management tools without manual design work.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of design skills stall your freelance projects. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for backend logic, you can generate production-ready project management dashboards. This workflow automates the aesthetic heavy lifting, allowing you to focus on shipping high-value features for clients faster.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer's worst nightmare when design isn't in your DNA. You have the logic, the architecture, and the client's requirements, but you're stuck staring at a pixel-perfect mockup you can't possibly replicate. The market doesn't care about your design degree; it cares about speed, reliability, and functional excellence.

Today, we pivot. We are moving away from manual styling and into the era of AI-orchestrated development. By coupling the rapid UI generation of Antigravity with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you can bypass the design phase entirely and deliver enterprise-grade project management tools that look like they were built by a dedicated design team.

steps.title: Define the Data Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, define your entities. A project management tool needs Tasks, Projects, and Users. Clarifying these relationships prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive schema for a project management SaaS including Tasks, Projects, and Users with relational constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create database/schema.sql based on the project management entity definitions'

steps.title: Scaffold the UI Layout
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modern dashboard layout. Focus on utility-first components to ensure responsiveness without custom CSS headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive project management dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS with a sidebar navigation and a Kanban board view.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'mkdir -p src/components && npx antigravity component --template dashboard'

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Sync your UI with your data. Ensure the Kanban board updates in real-time when a task status changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React state management hook for handling Kanban board drag-and-drop operations with local storage persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate the state management hook into the Kanban dashboard component'

steps.title: Automate API Integration
steps.description: Connect your frontend to your backend logic. Claude Code excels at mapping API endpoints to your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an API client for the project management backend to handle CRUD operations for tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate axios service for task management API'

steps.title: Polishing & Responsive Checks
steps.description: Finalize the look and feel. Use AI to scan for mobile responsiveness and accessibility issues.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current dashboard UI for accessibility and suggest Tailwind utility classes to improve mobile spacing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor CSS to ensure 100% mobile responsiveness across all dashboard views'

pro_tips: Always define your design system tokens (colors, spacing) in a central config file before generating components to ensure visual consistency.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on every generated file to catch potential security vulnerabilities in your API routes.
Prioritize 'functional minimalism'—if a feature isn't core to project management, don't build it. Clients pay for results, not bloat.
Use Antigravity's component library for standard UI elements but keep your 'Business Logic' components unique to your specific client needs.
Commit your code after every successful Claude Code run; this creates a granular rollback history that is invaluable when debugging complex state logic.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a platform like Vercel or Railway, gather client feedback, and use that feedback to iterate using Claude Code's CLI to implement requested changes in real-time.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build professional-grade tools regardless of your design background. Stop overthinking and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
